target.name: Alef Aeronautics
target.founder: Jim Dukhovny, Constantine Kisly, Pavel Markin, Oleg Petrov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Alef Aeronautics is a Series A-stage transportation company that has raised $30M+ to date. The transportation sector peer universe comprises eight private companies at various stages, including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger. No public comparables exist in this nascent subsector, limiting benchmark availability.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $30M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-subsequent-round progression yields an implied valuation of $60M. The Peer-Median method is not applicable: while five same-sector peers exist in Transportation, the narrow filter for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints required for statistical reliability. A broad sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of transportation technologies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median point of $60M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar—stage-based progression—rather than cross-validation with peer trading or transaction multiples. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next funding event, which will ultimately reset valuation through market price-discovery, and the absence of public market comps to anchor long-term value expectations. Additional specificity would require visibility into customer commitments, technical milestones, or peer funding events at comparable development stages.
